name: GPT-5.3-Codex
description: OpenAI's agentic coding specialist, still active with no announced shutdown (as of 2026-07-09): ~80% SWE-bench Verified, 77.3% Terminal-Bench, SOTA on SWE-Bench Pro at release, ~25% faster than GPT-5.2-Codex. The 5.3 generation shipped no general-purpose API flagship — only this Codex model plus a gpt-5.3-chat-latest ChatGPT alias that shuts down 2026-08-10.

evidence.value: ~80% resolution rate on SWE-bench Verified
77.3% on terminal/command-line agentic tasks
State-of-the-art on SWE-Bench Pro at release

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Industry-standard agentic coding benchmarks measuring real-world software engineering tasks

evidence.value: Strong code-centric reasoning; not positioned for general scientific or mathematical reasoning

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Reasoning benchmark review relative to general-purpose flagships

evidence.value: Specialized for software engineering; OpenAI recommends GPT-5.x flagships for general-purpose tasks

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Comparison against general-purpose models on non-coding workloads

evidence.value: ~25% faster than GPT-5.2-Codex with more reliable long-horizon task completion

output_consistency.methodology: Provider-reported reliability on multi-step agentic coding sessions

evidence.value: ~25% end-to-end speedup over GPT-5.2-Codex on equivalent tasks

latency_p50.methodology: Provider-reported relative latency on agentic coding workloads

evidence.value: 400K token context window; 128K max output tokens (official specification)
99.9% uptime (last 90 days)

uptime.methodology: Historical uptime data from official status page

performance_reliability.notes: Best-in-class agentic coding at release (~80% SWE-bench Verified, 77.3% Terminal-Bench, SOTA SWE-Bench Pro). Specialized model — general-purpose accuracy intentionally trails flagships.

evidence.value: Injection defenses tuned for agentic coding (repository content, tool output, shell results)

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Testing against OWASP LLM01 attacks including coding-agent vectors

evidence.value: Inherits GPT-5.x safety training with coding-specific refusal calibration (e.g., malware requests)

jailbreak_resistance.methodology: Adversarial prompt testing against jailbreak datasets

evidence.value: No training on API data by default

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of privacy policies and data handling practices

evidence.value: Sandboxed execution defaults in Codex environments; guardrails on destructive commands

output_safety.methodology: Safety testing across harmful content and dangerous-action categories

evidence.value: API key + OAuth2 authentication, HTTPS only, rate limiting

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features and best practices

security.notes: Good security posture with sandboxing in Codex environments. Autonomous code execution warrants strict permissioning and review gates in production pipelines.

evidence.value: Data residency options for enterprise customers
API data (including submitted code) not used for training by default

training_data_optout.methodology: Policy review of data usage terms

evidence.value: 30-day default API log retention; zero-data-retention options for qualifying customers

data_retention.methodology: Terms of service and enterprise documentation review

evidence.value: Customer responsible for scrubbing secrets/PII from code context; moderation API available
SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR compliant
Zero-data-retention options available for enterprise and qualifying API customers

privacy_compliance.notes: Standard OpenAI enterprise posture. Proprietary source code sent as context is covered by no-training-by-default; zero-data-retention recommended for sensitive codebases.

evidence.value: Step-by-step agent transcripts (plans, diffs, test runs) provide strong action-level traceability

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning and action transparency

evidence.value: Test-driven agent loop catches many fabrications; API hallucination still possible in unfamiliar frameworks

hallucination_rate.methodology: Code correctness evaluation with execution-based verification

evidence.value: Standard bias testing program; less salient for code-specialist deployments
Agent flags failing tests and unresolved tasks rather than claiming success

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment of confidence expression in agentic outputs

evidence.value: Release documentation covers benchmarks, intended use, and Codex-only scope of the 5.3 generation
General description of code-focused training; specific sources not disclosed

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Guardrails on destructive operations, secrets handling, and malware generation

trust_transparency.notes: Agent transcripts give strong action-level auditability. Execution-based verification reduces unchecked hallucination relative to chat-style code generation.

evidence.value: Responses API plus first-class integration with Codex CLI, IDE extensions, and Codex cloud

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design, consistency, and feature completeness

evidence.value: Official SDKs plus open-source Codex CLI, actively maintained

sdk_quality.methodology: SDK quality, documentation, and maintenance review

evidence.value: Clear deprecation schedule; predecessor GPT-5.2-Codex shuts down 2026-07-23

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of versioning policy and deprecation practices

evidence.value: Detailed usage dashboard with costs, tokens, rate limits; Codex task history

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of available monitoring tools and metrics

evidence.value: 24/7 support, comprehensive docs, active developer community
Codex CLI, IDE extensions, cloud agents, GitHub integration; also available via OpenRouter
Standard commercial terms; customer retains rights to generated code per terms of use

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and restrictions

operational_excellence.notes: Deep coding-tool ecosystem (CLI, IDE, cloud agents). Codex line moves fast: GPT-5.2-Codex shuts down 2026-07-23, so plan for shorter model lifecycles than general flagships.

code-generation.notes: Purpose-built agentic coder: ~80% SWE-bench Verified, 77.3% Terminal-Bench, SOTA SWE-Bench Pro at release, ~25% faster than GPT-5.2-Codex.

data-analysis.notes: Strong at writing and executing analysis code; general flagships better for open-ended analytical interpretation.

research-assistant.notes: Useful for code-centric research (reproducing papers, building experiment harnesses); not designed for general literature work.

education.notes: Excellent for programming instruction with executable, test-verified examples; narrow outside software topics.

strengths: ~80% SWE-bench Verified and SOTA on SWE-Bench Pro at release
~25% faster than GPT-5.2-Codex on equivalent workloads
Aggressive pricing (~$1.75/$14 per 1M) for a frontier coding model
Deep tooling: Codex CLI, IDE extensions, cloud agents, GitHub integration
Execution-verified outputs reduce unchecked code hallucination
limitations: Specialized for coding — weaker than flagships on general reasoning and writing
No general-purpose GPT-5.3 API flagship; only the Codex model and a gpt-5.3-chat-latest alias (shutdown 2026-08-10)
Fast Codex lifecycle: predecessor GPT-5.2-Codex shuts down 2026-07-23, suggesting shorter support horizons
Autonomous code execution requires sandboxing and review gates
best_for: Agentic software engineering: multi-step bug fixes, refactors, and feature implementation
CI/automation pipelines needing fast, cost-efficient frontier coding
Teams migrating off GPT-5.2-Codex before its 2026-07-23 shutdown
not_recommended_for: General-purpose chat, writing, or research assistants
Teams requiring multi-year model stability without migrations

pricing.notes: Confirmed on OpenAI's official pricing and model pages 2026-07-09. Cached input $0.175 per 1M; Priority tier $3.50/$28.00 per 1M.

metadata.architecture: Transformer-based, fine-tuned for agentic software engineering (Codex line)
